name: Progressive Link
preceding_figure_names: Back Corte, Basic Reverse Turn, Brush Tap, Chase, Closed Finish, Closed Promenade, Fallaway Reverse and Slip Pivot, Four Step Change, Open Finish, Open Promenade, Open Reverse Turn, Lady In Line, Open Reverse Turn, Lady Outside, Outside Swivel, Progressive Side Step Reverse Turn, Promenade Link, Reverse Outside Swivel, Rock Turn, Walk
following_figure_names: Closed Promenade, Open Promenade, Natural Twist Turn, Natural Promenade Turn, Promenade Link, Back Open Promenade, Fallaway Promenade, Chase
notes: Note: The figure has solid, sharp feeling, not soft. It is more of an "upward" action with body projecting upward. 90 body rotation in body seems to give the right look and impact. Promenade position: Lady's L hip feels like rolled downward and backward, R hip fwd stretched, toward Man. Hip is level, Lady may feel R hip is higher. Hips has an diagonal angle to Man. L hip is "released". Upper body is closed more. Actions are in hips, nothing should affect top shoulder line, arm relaxed, no increased tension. Even when doing with full power, arms should not be involved. When moving out of PP, LF fwd, legs are moving, hips don't change, L hip stays in "bent/release" position, don't flatten out L hip when stepping LF fwd. As described above, the link starts in DW. But often, link is preceded by 2 walks which started DW. Walks naturally curve to left, so the Link will end up with Man's feet facing LOD, Woman's feet facing center., traveling DC.

steps.action: LF forward in CBMP | DW | H | No Turn | No CBM
comments: very small step, very strong CBMP feel, very tight
using inside L knee next to outside of Lady's L knee
mini contra check
feeling the LF is slightly turned in to help with the rotation into link
Create torque to wind up lady before turning her
Turn the lady to Right, not Left
Man steps beneath the lady
There is the effect of a windup before the second step
Keep shoulder flat, do not pop.

steps.action: RF side to side and slightly back in PP | Facing DW | IE of foot and IE of B of LF | 1/4 to R | No CBM
comments: release tension of CBM of the last step
RF steps under right shoulder, frame turns sharply right
rotate thigh and hips first, then upper torso catches up, but elbow never goes behind body
Lady is in front Man, but behind right hip
Frame is turning to R at the same time to lead lady into a link
Make it quick and short
Head should always be at the same level
Avoid the tendency to lower on a walk, and then rise back up on a side step
Knees coming together, flexed
do not raise right shoulder, expand across chest, maintain big frame

steps.action: RF back in CBMP | BDW | BH | No Turn | No CBM
comments: Start with winding up to left (rotate L hip to L, R hip fwd)
RF strong CBMP, crossing in the back more to give Man room
front of lower R thigh against back lower L thigh tightly
RF follows where center moves
R toe in, outside edge first, small step
Start rotating to R after more than half weight is committed
After putting Ball of RF down, R heel will kiss the floor before transfer weight to LF
Stay very left behind in Man's R arm throughout
Hip is quite closed up to Man
Center is to Man
To increase impact, turn left first then sharp turn to R
It's not just placement of the foot, but also shift of the center
PP: pick up R side, project to Man

steps.action: LF side, slightly back in PP | Facing DC | IE BH of LF and IE of B of RF | 1/4 to R |
comments: position is slightly back in relation to the body (after 1/4 turn), not LOD
Body is more or less facing wall, Hip FDC
Depending on the direction Man wants to go, this could be either almost in place or side/slightly back step
Stay on Man's R, stepping a bigger step, to end behind Man in promenade position
Swivel on Ball of LF
Hip turn 1/4 to R, but body turns 1/8
Keep body as still as possible
Rib and hip should rotate 1/8 relative to each other
Knees more together, not open
L hip is higher (but not in a way falling forward)
If R hip is higher, it makes one heavy and slow, more difficult to move
You want to dance off the ball of foot, not heel of the foot
LF should be on the IE of the foot, 45 degrees (DC) off the line of travel (LOD), parallel to RF
RF is one floor board off to R, giving it room for Man and Lady to step through in the middle
Bring R side to Man
Upward feeling
Extend - from 7th vertebra bend back
Connection with Man moves lower to hip/leg
Do not turn away from Man
Right shoulder needs to stay with Man, try to get shoulder to square with Man
Do not turn shoulder out, otherwise, it cancels the effect of extension
R hand: do not bend wrist back, wrist forward
L hand: maintain "up and out" position, do not slide
Head turning: should not be on its own, shouldn't be too late or too early
Turning the head when weight is changing onto LF
Head change finishes when the action finishes
